description: Track direction fix, speed physics alignment, ranking on title screen, AdSense slot on game over, and player feedback modal.

userStories.title: Corrigir direção do scroll da pista
userStories.description: As a player, I want the road to scroll toward me (top to bottom) so that the game feels like I'm driving forward and not in reverse.
acceptanceCriteria: Lane dashes scroll from top to bottom during gameplay (y position increases each frame)
Rumble strips animate from top to bottom
Road horizon perspective effect remains at the top of the road area
Opponents spawn at top of screen and move downward — never upward
scrollOffset increases monotonically frame to frame — never decrements during normal gameplay

REGRESSION after implementing — navigate to game over screen and verify all of these: (1) 'GAME OVER' text renders visible at canvas y ≈ 270; (2) Stats Time, Distance, Score show values > 0; (3) Ranking panel appears at y ≈ 537+ with status loading → loaded or error; (4) #name-form appears over canvas without covering 'GAME OVER'; (5) Pressing Enter transitions to playingState without console errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Alinhar física de velocidade — pista mais rápida que adversários
userStories.description: As a player, I want the road to scroll visibly faster than opponent cars so that I feel like I am overtaking them as I accelerate.
acceptanceCriteria: Opponent visual displacement per frame = scrollSpeed × (1 - speedRatio), always positive (downward)
Sedan (speedRatio 0.6): moves visibly slower than road — player appears to overtake it
Truck (speedRatio 0.4): moves very slowly — heavy obstacle feel
Sports Car (speedRatio 0.85): nearly matches player speed — hard to overtake
Motorcycle (speedRatio 0.7): medium speed — weaves laterally
At no scrollSpeed value (200→800 px/s) does an opponent move upward or stay completely static
Road acceleration (scrollSpeed ramp) is visibly reflected in perceived speed increase
REGRESSION after implementing — repeat game over checklist from US-001: GAME OVER text, stats, ranking panel, name form, Enter → playingState without console errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Extrair função renderRankingPanel para reutilização
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ranking rendering logic extracted into a standalone function so that both the title screen and game over screen can use it without duplicating code.
acceptanceCriteria: Create function renderRankingPanel(ctx, rankingData, rankingStatus, rankingScroll, rankingDotTime, highlightName) that draws the ranking section on a Canvas 2D context
gameOverState.render() delegates the ranking section to renderRankingPanel — visual output is identical to before (no regression)
Function accepts a highlightName parameter (string) to highlight a specific row in gold
Visually verify game over screen looks identical after extraction (same layout, same colors, same rows)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Botão RANKING e overlay na tela de título
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the global leaderboard from the title screen so that I can check rankings without needing to play a game first.
acceptanceCriteria: HTML button #ranking-btn created once on startup (not recreated on each transition), visible only in titleState
Button text '🏆 RANKING'; styled as glassmorphism pill (semi-transparent dark background, white text, border-radius 20px); min-height 44px; min-width 140px; font-size 16px
positionRankingBtn() positions button at canvas logical y ≈ 440, centered horizontally — called inside resizeCanvas()
titleState.onEnter() shows #ranking-btn; titleState.onExit() hides #ranking-btn
titleRankingState.onEnter() hides #ranking-btn
playingState, explosionState and gameOverState keep #ranking-btn hidden via their onEnter()
Pressing R/r in titleState OR clicking/touching #ranking-btn triggers fsm.transition(titleRankingState)
titleRankingState.onEnter() always resets: rankingData = [], rankingStatus = 'loading', rankingScroll = 0, rankingDotTime = 0 — never shows stale data from previous open
Fetch uses AbortController: if a previous fetch is still pending when onEnter() is called again, abort it before starting new fetch
Fetch has 10-second timeout via AbortController + setTimeout — if exceeded, rankingStatus = 'error' with message 'Timeout — tente novamente'
titleRankingState renders: loading state with animated dots, error state with retry hint, or ranking panel via renderRankingPanel(ctx, ..., localStorage.getItem('roadRushPlayerName') || '')

HTML button #ranking-back-btn created once: text 'VOLTAR', min-height 44px, font-size 16px, visible only in titleRankingState, positioned at canvas logical y ≈ 660 via positionRankingBackBtn() called in resizeCanvas()
Tapping/clicking #ranking-back-btn OR pressing ESC in titleRankingState transitions to titleState
Opening overlay 3 consecutive times always shows loading + new fetch — no instant stale data on third open
Overlay is dismissable after timeout (ESC and VOLTAR still work after 10s timeout)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — test: title → overlay (wait for loading) → VOLTAR → title → overlay again (must reload)

userStories.title: Espaço reservado para anúncio AdSense no game over
userStories.description: As the game owner, I want a soft contextual ad slot displayed within the game over screen so that I can enable AdSense revenue after approval, without disrupting the play experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Add AdSense script to index.html <head> with comment: '<!-- AdSense: replace XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX with your Publisher ID after approval -->'

Add
to index.html body after canvas, with comment indicating where to paste AdSense unit code
div#ad-gameover does NOT have pointer-events:none — it must be clickable when active (required by AdSense policy)
Add label 'PUBLICIDADE' in 10px above the ad slot (AdSense requirement)
Create positionGameOverAd() using canvas.getBoundingClientRect() to center #ad-gameover at canvas logical y ≈ 90–210, max-width 300px — called inside resizeCanvas()
Create showGameOverAd(): reads sessionStorage 'roadrush_game_count', shows ad when count is even (0, 2, 4...); increments count; calls positionGameOverAd()
Create hideGameOverAd(): sets #ad-gameover display:none
gameOverState.onEnter() calls showGameOverAd(); gameOverState.onExit() calls hideGameOverAd()
Ad does not overlap: 'GAME OVER' text (logical y 0–80), name form (y ~350–480), ranking panel (y ~537+)
On mobile (screen width <= 480px): ad max-width is min(300px, 90vw) so it does not overflow canvas
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Modal de feedback — sugestão de melhorias
userStories.description: As a player, I want to suggest game improvements and leave my name so that I can be notified when my suggestion is implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: Add constant FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_URL = 'https://n8n.ai-solutions.startse.com/webhook/e6c46e71-f564-4e8b-b6bd-041ca8f012e0/feedback' near SCORE_WEBHOOK_URL in main.js

Create HTML button #feedback-btn created once: text '💡 Sugerir Melhoria'; pill style (rgba(255,193,7,0.15) background, #FFC107 text, border-radius 20px); min-height 44px; font-size 16px; min-width 160px
positionFeedbackBtn() positions button at canvas logical y ≈ 640, centered — called inside resizeCanvas()
#feedback-btn visible in titleState and gameOverState; hidden in playingState and explosionState via their onEnter()
Clicking/touching #feedback-btn opens #feedback-modal overlay
Modal subtitle uses realistic language — does NOT promise automatic AI implementation. Use: 'Sua sugestão será analisada pela equipe. Deixe seu nome para ser avisado quando for implementado!'

Modal contains: title '💡 Sugira uma Melhoria', subtitle (realistic), textarea #feedback-text (placeholder 'O que você gostaria de ver no jogo?', maxLength 500, rows 4), input #feedback-name (placeholder 'Seu nome (para te avisar quando for implementado)', maxLength 40), error span #feedback-error, submit button #feedback-submit ('Enviar Sugestão'), close button X
All text inputs use font-size >= 16px; modal card uses max-height: 90vh; overflow-y: auto
#feedback-name pre-filled with localStorage.getItem('roadRushPlayerName') on modal open if available
keydown on textarea and name input calls e.stopPropagation() to prevent game key handling; ESC closes modal without submitting
Clicking dark overlay backdrop closes modal without submitting
Submit validates both fields non-empty; shows #feedback-error message if either is blank

On submit: POST JSON {name, suggestion} to FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_URL; button shows 'Enviando...' and is disabled; on success shows 'Obrigado! ✓' then closes modal after 1500ms; on error shows 'Erro. Tentar novamente' and re-enables button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
